text:
Market today: Mood is cautious-to-defensive. Investors are rotating OUT of tech into healthcare, tariff threats on digital services are spooking big-cap tech, and geopolitical noise (Iran/Hormuz) is adding risk-off pressure. That's a headwind for 90% of your book.

Your holdings: AVGO is the urgent one — down 16% and flagged SELL as your largest position; on margin that hole is real and growing. PLTR is down nearly 13% and also flagged SELL — two big red flags at once. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history; it's already -0.57% with a tight stop at $150 — treat it as hair-trigger. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: (1) Whether the digital-services tariff threat broadens — it could hit tech sentiment hard and fast. (2) AVGO price action near your $336 stop — a break there on margin is a forced-decision moment.

Bottom line: Two SELL-flagged positions (your biggest) bleeding on margin — that's the only thing that matters today.
